implemented_slices.summary: AS now consumes single input-channel init-family command messages on fixed and stem recipient cells while leaving non-init command-message inputs blocked.

implemented_claims.summary: AS now has a named transition claim and proof-certificate entry for the recipient init-family command-message consumption subset.

implemented_traces.summary: AS now has a schematic-linked trace that replays fixed-cell upstream recipient init command-message consumption.

implemented_svgs.summary: AS now has a rendered SVG view checked exactly against the recipient init command-message trace.

implemented_source_statuses.summary: AS now has a source-status decision blocking recipient-side non-init command-message execution and selecting a rejection-boundary claim as the safe next slice.
AS now has a source-status decision keeping write-buffer command execution blocked across recipient, self-mailbox, and self-target command-buffer surfaces.
AS now has a source-status decision keeping standard-signal command-token execution blocked across recipient, self-mailbox, and self-target command-buffer surfaces.
AS now has a source-status decision selecting reject-and-clear for multiple simultaneous recipient command-message inputs.

formal_model_input_special_message_ancho.summary: The formal model routes input-channel special messages on wire, proc, and stem cells through process-special-message after clearing input-channel state.
formal_model_input_special_message_ancho.as_interpretation: Recipient-side command-message inputs should be executable behavior, but only after each command subset has a source-backed boundary.

formal_stem_self_mailbox_anchor.summary: The formal model's stem-process-special-message path clears the self mailbox and then executes the special message; stem-init is the explicit shown case.
formal_stem_self_mailbox_anchor.as_interpretation: AS already uses this source pressure for self-mailbox init-family command execution.

legacy_special_message_witnesses.summary: RAA names seven special messages, processes input-channel special messages, and maps init-family plus write-buffer messages to primitive operations.
The semantic simulator names the same seven special messages and maps stem input special messages to init-family and write-buffer operations.
The FSM simulator treats special-message input as process-special-message regardless of output occupancy, then dispatches init-family and write-buffer operations.

allowed_next_slice.summary: AS may next implement recipient-side input-channel consumption for init-family command messages only: stem-init, wire-r-init, wire-l-init, proc-r-init, and proc-l-init.

blockers.summary: ADR-0058 records standard-signal command-token execution as blocked: the formal command table includes standard-signal at offset 0, but ordinary standard-signal behavior is binary-input behavior and legacy special-message sets exclude standard-signal.
Legacy sources define write-buf-zero and write-buf-one primitive operations, but AS has not yet selected a source-backed write-buffer boundary for fixed cells, stem cells, and buffer-full behavior.
ADR-0059 selects reject-and-clear for multiple simultaneous command-message inputs; ADR-0060 adds the schematic-linked rejection trace, and ADR-0061 adds the rendered SVG view.

allowed_next_slices: Revisit standard-signal command execution only if new source evidence resolves command-token, binary-input, recipient-surface, and self-target-surface boundaries.
Revisit write-buffer command execution only if new source evidence resolves append, clearing, and buffer-full boundaries.
